enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps his Saturday, describing staying up late and then spending the day working on a personal mapping/geography project that creates 'sister cities to Rome' centered on Boulder, Colorado — matching distances proportionally to map Roman Empire geography onto the Boulder area, with plans to eventually recreate all Roman Empire territories.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Enthusiastically treating Boulder as the Rome counterpart for a personal geographic mapping project
Wants to recreate a counterpart to everything in the Roman Empire, going all-in on the project

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He got up at around 10 AM on November 19, 2016.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He was up until 4 AM on November 19, 2016.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He got 6 hours of sleep on November 19, 2016.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He started building sister cities to Rome, with Boulder as the counterpart to Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He began with the cities farthest out from Rome and noted their proximity percentages.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He kept the sizes of the sister cities about the same as their Roman counterparts.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He focused on the more interesting sister cities and completed the smaller ones closer to Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is now working on the sister cities in Italy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to create tribe areas and Roman States based on the sister cities.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wants to create a counterpart to everything in the Roman Empire.

transcript.text: Okay, hey, how's it going? Living in Boulder Vlog. Today's Sunday, so this one's going to be for Saturday the 19th of November.

So, um, so yesterday what I did was I got up at like 9 or something. I can't remember. I was up until, oh right, I was up until 4 and then I got 6 hours of sleep. So I got up at like 10. It's only like my second or third end of the day.
Okay. So yesterday I just got going on building sister cities to Rome, man. You know, like all roads lead to Rome. So all roads lead to Boulder because Boulder's Boulder Rome.
Um, so I just started with the ones farthest out, you know, and it was like, if it's the same distance away, you know, within 90% and I, and I, I write down what percentage of closest they are.

I wish one's bigger, but, um, yeah, I keep them about the same. And then I kind of, I didn't get all of them, but I kind of reined it in on the interesting ones and just did it all, you know, and did the little ones close.
And now I'm doing the ones in Italy. So, and off of that, I can then create the tribe areas and the, uh, Roman States and stuff.
I want to make a counterpart to everything in the Roman Empire, man. Just get totally, uh, just go for all of it.
I don't do it all because I think I can get away with it. Oh shit. All right. So that's enough.
